title: Map Command, Page State, Navigation Invariants, and Transactional Updates · Execution Trace: Pokedex Go Map Transition Tests
summary: The map command prints names but loses the page identity, advances before a request succeeds, permits negative offsets, and cannot explain what back should restore. Predict the user decision, Go ownership, command state, API contract, resource budget, failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pokedex go map transition tests increment in transactional Pokemon page navigator. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, ch…
objectives: Prove first, middle, last, empty, short, changed-count, failed-fetch, and repeated-command state invariants.
Number results, preserve canonical identity, show page position when count is known, and remain readable at narrow widths.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
